nodes.label: Kevin Knuth
nodes.summary: Kevin Hunter Knuth (born 1965) is a Professor of Physics at the University at Albany (SUNY). Knuth conducts research in information physics, foundations of quantum mechanics, and Bayesian analysis with applications towards various problems in physics. He also conducts research into UFOs.

nodes.label: 1988 Bozeman Cattle Mutilation + UFO Reports
nodes.summary: September 1988 incident at Montana ranch near Bozeman — two cows killed, blood drained, sensory organs and genitals removed, one with a cylindrical-core-sample hole punched through it. Hundreds of UFO reports in the county same night. Knuth's formative UAP encounter.

sources.timestamp_end: Two cows surgically manipulated with cylindrical core sample; hundreds of UFO reports same night

nodes.label: Underwater UAP Bases Hypothesis
nodes.summary: Hypothesis (per Knuth and many witnesses): UAPs emerging from water, following ships, centuries of sightings — most likely explanation is UAP bases underwater.

sources.timestamp_end: UAP phenomena around water suggest underwater bases

nodes.label: Apollo 12 and Skylab astronaut Alan Bean told Knuth that on Skylab they actually photographed a red
nodes.summary: Apollo 12 and Skylab astronaut Alan Bean told Knuth that on Skylab they actually photographed a red flashing light — 'nobody puts lights on satellites, you don't need them, it's more weight.'

sources.quote: I talked to Alan Bean. He was from Apollo 12. When he went up to Skylab, they actually photographed a red flashing light. Nobody puts lights on satellites.

type_specific.statement_text: Apollo 12 and Skylab astronaut Alan Bean told Knuth that on Skylab they actually photographed a red flashing light — 'nobody puts lights on satellites, you don't need them, it's more weight.'

nodes.label: Per Knuth: a Russian cosmonaut drew a picture of an object that pulled up alongside his craft and ma
nodes.summary: Per Knuth: a Russian cosmonaut drew a picture of an object that pulled up alongside his craft and matched their orbit.

sources.quote: They actually had a craft pull up alongside of them. They had their orbit. And the cosmonaut drew a picture of the object.

type_specific.statement_text: Per Knuth: a Russian cosmonaut drew a picture of an object that pulled up alongside his craft and matched their orbit.

nodes.label: Shuttle astronaut Story Musgrave reported seeing 'snakes writhing around in space' during missions —

nodes.label: Knuth's formative UAP experience: September 1988 at Montana State University — two ranch cows surgic
nodes.summary: Knuth's formative UAP experience: September 1988 at Montana State University — two ranch cows surgically manipulated (cylindrical core sample, blood drained, organs removed), coincident with hundreds of UFO reports in the county same night.

sources.quote: Our first week or two there, there was a cattle mutilation. Two cows were killed and surgically manipulated. The blood was drained, the sensory organs removed, the genitals removed. One of them had like a core sample. A cylindrical hole punched through it. There were UFO reports in the county that night, hundreds of reports.

type_specific.statement_text: Knuth's formative UAP experience: September 1988 at Montana State University — two ranch cows surgically manipulated (cylindrical core sample, blood drained, organs removed), coincident with hundreds of UFO reports in the county same night.

nodes.label: Knuth: UAPs emerging from water, hovering by ships, and flying into clouds has been documented since
nodes.summary: Knuth: UAPs emerging from water, hovering by ships, and flying into clouds has been documented since the 1800s — 'that's why I think there's probably bases underwater is your best bet.'

sources.quote: This is a phenomena that people have seen for centuries. Balls of light coming out of the water, hovering next to the ship, following the ship, and then taking off into the clouds. I think there's probably bases underwater is your best bet.

type_specific.statement_text: Knuth: UAPs emerging from water, hovering by ships, and flying into clouds has been documented since the 1800s — 'that's why I think there's probably bases underwater is your best bet.'

sources.quote: Why when we had the NASA commission, why was there no section on what astronauts have seen in space?
